data.title: GSK shares break six-session winning streak
data.description:
GSK (NYSE:GSK) shares snapped six straight sessions of gains, as the stock ended down 0.6% at $38.70 on Friday.

The pharmaceutical giant added 3.6% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has gained over 14% so far this year, compared to a 0.7% drop in the broader S&P 500 Index.

GSK is up 3% over the past one month.

Earlier in the day, GSK announced that an expert panel of the EU drug regulator endorsed its antibody-drug conjugate Blenrep as a late-line option and as part of a combination regimen for adults with multiple myeloma, a type of blood cancer.

On Thursday, the U.S. FDA further broadened the label for GSK's asthma therapy, approving the injection as an add-on treatment for certain adults with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.

GSK is also set to acquire Boston Pharmaceuticals’ lead asset, efimosfermin alfa, for as much as $2B in cash.

 
 

"GSK's healthy dividend yield and the company's long history of payouts are particularly attractive at the present uncertain times," pointed out a Seeking Alpha analysis.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, GSK has a Hold rating with a score of 3.4 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, A- in momentum, while it got an F growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, two analysts gave GSK a Strong Buy rating. Four analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while two recommended Sell or lower.

Seeking Alpha analysts are positive and see the stock as a Buy.

data.title: Liquidia's Yutrepia gets FDA approval for added indication
data.description:
Liquidia Corporation (NASDAQ:LQDA) said on Friday that the U.S. FDA has approved Yutrepia inhalation powder, a prostacyclin analog, for adults with pulmonary arterial hypertension and pulmonary hypertension linked to interstitial lung disease.

Earlier, United Therapeutics (UTHR) had filed a lawsuit and requested a court order to block Liquidia from launching Yutrepia, claiming it infringes on a patent. A hearing was held on May 20, and the motion remains pending with the court.

Post the approval, Liquidia said that it will host a webcast Tuesday, May 27, to provide an update on commercial launch preparations.

The approval is based on data from the late stage INSPIRE trial, which included both patients new to treprostinil therapy and those switching from nebulized versions.

data.title: Informatica jumps as Salesforce in talks to acquire company: report
data.description:
• Informatica (NYSE:INFA) shares jumped more than 25% on Friday as Bloomberg reported that Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) is in talks to acquire the enterprise cloud software company.
  • A deal could be announced as soon as next week, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter.
  • Salesforce shares fell 3% following the report.
  • The Marc Benioff-led company had talks to acquire Informatica in 2024, but the deal fell through.

This is breaking news...

data.title: Boeing to avoid prosecution over 737 MAX crashes in deal with Justice Department
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Justice said Friday it reached a deal in principle with Boeing (NYSE:BA) that allows the company to avoid criminal prosecution for allegedly misleading U.S. regulators about two fatal MAX plane crashes that killed 346 people.

Under the agreement, Boeing (NYSE:BA) must "pay or invest" more than $1.1B, the DoJ said in its filing in federal court in Texas, including a $487.2M criminal fine - although $243.6M it already paid in an earlier agreement would be credited - $445M for a new fund for crash victims, and another $445M on compliance, safety and quality programs.

The agreement is a blow to families who lost relatives in the crashes and had pressed prosecutors to take Boeing (BA) to trial.

The deal still needs to be finalized, and the DoJ expects to file the written agreement with Boeing (BA) by the end of next week; the company has not yet commented on the deal.

data.title: Oracle plans to spend $40B on Nvidia chips to power OpenAI data center: report
data.description:
Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) plans to spend about $40B on Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) high-end chips to power OpenAI's new Texas data center, according to a report by the Financial Times.

The data center will be the first constructed as part of the $500B U.S. Stargate Project, according to the report. The 1.2 GW data center will be located in Abilene, Texas, and will be one of the largest in the world. Oracle will purchase 400,000 of Nvidia's GB200 chips for training and running artificial intelligence applications. The new data center is expected to start operations by the middle of 2026. 

The Stargate Project was officially announced in January by U.S. President Donald Trump, along with SoftBank Group (OTCPK:SFTBY), Oracle and OpenAI. Arm (ARM), Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and Nvidia were also named as key partners in the project. SoftBank and OpenAI have each contributed $18B to Stargate. 

Earlier this week, it was announced that OpenAI, Nvidia, Oracle, SoftBank, Cisco Systems (CSCO) and G42 are collaborating to build Stargate UAE. The 1-GW compute cluster will be built by G42 and operated by OpenAI and Oracle. That project will also utilize Nvidia's Grace Blackwell chips.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Eyes on Nvidia, OPEC, and box office blockbusters
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - May 26

  • All day - The U.S. stock market is closed for observance of the Memorial Day holiday.
  • All day - The U.S. box office numbers for the extended four-day holiday weekend will be reported. Disney's (DIS) live-action Lilo & Stitch is expected to bring in between $185 million to $200 million, while Paramount's (PARA) Mission: Impossible – The Final Reckoning is forecast to see a tally in the $75 million and $85 million range.

 

Tuesday - May 27

  • Volatility watch - Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) and UroGen Pharma (URGN) have a higher level of implied volatility than normal based on options trading. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include NervGen Pharma (OTCQB:NGENF), SatixFy Communications (SATX), and Regulus Therapeutics (RGLS). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Spirit Aviation (FLYY), Compass Diversified (CODI), and B&G Foods (BGS).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include AutoZone (AZO), Okta (OKTA), and Box (BOX).
  • Fed watch - Minneapolis Federal Reserve Bank President Neel Kashkari speaks and participates in a policy panel before the Bank of Japan Institute for Monetary and Economic Studies Conference. New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will participate in a moderated discussion panel before the Bank of Japan Institute for Monetary and Economic Studies Conference.
  • All day - The three-day Bitcoin 2025 Conference will feature talks by Vice President JD Vance, Strategy (MSTR) CEO Michael Saylor, and Robinhood Markets (HOOD) CEO Vlad Tenev.
  • All day - PENN Entertainment (PENN), Oklo (OKLO), and DraftKings (DKNG) are some of the notable companies due to present at the Craig-Hallum Institutional Investor Conference.
  • All day - Aya Gold & Silver (AYA:CA) (OTCQX:AYASF) will present at the Raymond James Silver Day.

 

Wednesday - May 28

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Nvidia (NVDA), Salesforce (CRM), Bank of Montreal (BMO), Agilent Technologies (A), HP (HPQ), Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS), and Pure Storage (PSTG). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for e.l.f. Beauty (ELF) and C3.ai (AI) after their reports are released.
  • All day - Zentalis Pharmaceuticals (ZNTL), Aura Biosciences (AURA), and Protara Therapeutics (TARA) are some of the companies due to present at the TD Cowen's Oncology Innovation Summit.
  • All day - The 60th session of the Joint Ministerial Monitoring Committee of OPEC and non-OPEC countries is scheduled to take place in Vienna, Austria. The committee will pay particular attention to the additional voluntary production adjustments made by eight OPEC+ countries, which were announced in April and November 2023, and are intended to support market stability.
  • All day - Other notable investor events include Coherent Corp.'s (COHR) Investor Day, Performance Food Group's (PFGC) Investor Day, Exxon Mobil's (XOM) annual meeting, First Advantage's (FA) Investor Day, and Chevron's (CVX) annual meeting.
  • All day - Horizon Aircraft (HOVR), Honeywell (HON), Blade Air Mobility (BLDE), and Surf Air Mobility (SRFM) are some of the companies confirmed to be participating in the Jefferies eVTOL Summit.
  • 8:30 a.m. AMD (AMD) will present at the TD Cowen Technology, Media and Telecom Conference.
  • 8:30 a.m. Visa (V) will present at the Bernstein Strategic Decisions Conference.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will release the minutes from the last meeting of the FOMC.

 

Thursday - May 29

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Costco Wholesale (COST), Royal Bank of Canada (RY), Dell Technologies (DELL), Marvell Technology (MRVL), Zscaler (ZS), and Li Auto (LI). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Caleres (CAL), Pagerduty (PD), and Starz (STRZ) after their reports are released.
  • All day - Ashland (ASH) will hold its Innovation Day for analysts and investors at the company’s Bridgewater, New Jersey facility. The event will showcase the enhanced capabilities and expanded applications of Ashland's scalable technology platforms.
  • 10:40 a.m. Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee participates in a moderated question-and-answer session before the 2025 Mackinac Policy Conference hosted by the Detroit Regional Chamber.
  • 4:00 p.m. San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary Daly will participate in a fireside chat before the Oakland Rotary Club.

 

Friday - May 30

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Shoe Carnival (SCVL).
  • All day - The five-day ASCO Conference will begin. The major oncology conference will include a flurry of abstracts and poster presentations that could impact share prices in the healthcare sector.
  • All day - EA Sports F1 25 will be released by Electronic Arts (EA) for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC.
  • All day - The Ira Sohn Conference Hong Kong will take place. The conference has led to share price jumps for some stocks in the past after hedge funds made pitches.
  • 8:30 a.m. The core PCE price index report for April will be released. The Fed's favored inflation gauge was up 2.6% year-over-year in March.
  • 7:30 p.m. Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee will participate in a moderated Q&A before "The Interview Show with Mark Bazer."

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story.

data.title: Trump signs executive orders aiming to expand nuclear power, speed up reactor construction
data.description:
President Trump signed executive orders Friday aimed at expanding the production of nuclear energy and easing the regulatory process on approvals for new nuclear reactors.

Stocks related to nuclear power and uranium production have been surging after Reuters reported late Thursday that the executive orders were coming: Lightbridge (LTBR) +43%, Nano Nuclear Energy (NNE) +31%, Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) +28.1%, Uranium Energy (UEC) +25.5%, Centrus Energy (LEU) +24.5%, Energy Fuels (UUUU) +19.6%, NuScale Power (SMR) +18.3%, NexGen Energy (NXE) +14.7%, ASP Isotopes (ASPI) +13.6%, Denison Mines (DNN) +11.3%, Cameco (CCJ) +10%, BWX Technologies (BWXT) +9.9%, Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG) +2.1%, Vistra (VST) +2.1%, Talen Energy (TLN) +1.7%, NRG Energy (NRG) +1.6%.

Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG) CEO Joseph Dominguez and Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) CEO Jacob DeWitte accompanied Trump and spoke at the signing.

The orders are intended to reform nuclear energy research at the U.S. Department of Energy, clear a path to allow the DoE to build nuclear reactors on federally-owned land, overhaul the Nuclear Regulatory Commission, and expand uranium mining and enrichment in the U.S.

ETFs: (URA), (NLR), (URNM)

data.title: Newmont continues rally for seventh session in a row
data.description:
Newmont (NYSE:NEM) was extending gains for the seventh straight session on Friday as its shares added 1.73% to reach $53.56 during afternoon trading.

The gold mining firm’s shares rose nearly 6% in the past six trading sessions. It closed almost flat at $52.65 on Thursday. It began its upward trajectory on May 15 after declining in the previous three sessions following the US-China trade deal. The stock was one of the five largest losers on the S&P 500 following the deal which dented gold’s appeal as a safe-haven asset.

Shortly after, the company was downgraded by Seeking Alpha analyst JR Research from Buy to Hold. They argued that Newmont has lagged behind the VanEck Gold Miners ETF (GDX) since July 2022, despite being the top constituent and benefiting from rising gold prices.

“Newmont's FCF margins are projected to stay strong, but elevated AISC and production challenges may hinder near-term performance,” the analyst added.

As per Seeking Alpha’s quant ratings, NEM has been rated a Strong Buy with a score of 4.81 out of 5 with an A+ for profitability and B+ for momentum. Wall Street as well as Seeking Alpha analysts issued a Buy rating for the stock.

Seeking Alpha analyst Eliana Scialabba believes that Newmont's valuation multiples are appealing, presenting a strong risk-reward opportunity for long-term investors.

“Newmont undervalues its real value when measured against what it does best: consistently generate cash, operate with margins well above the industry average and maintain a strong financial structure. When I compare its multipliers linked to the actual profits with those of other mining companies, I consider that its price is more than reasonable for the quality of the business it offers,” she said, rating it a Buy.
